creator: spamtrap@null.net
text:
Let me explain for gaming with 2 Xbox's. Currently 2 Xbox's on the same console can establish an open NAT with the Xbox Live service since the Xbox Live service requires only one port to properly work. In the Xbox settings you can set a specific static port to use for Live services. Both Xbox's would then have their own separate static port and won't change unless you manually change it. So establishing an open NAT with more than one Xbox is currently possible after setting 2 rules for each static Xbox Live port and setting static-port for outbound NAT.

Gaming is completely separate from the Xbox Live service. You can only forward specific ports for a game to one single Xbox on a LAN network. It's currently not possible to do this with more than one Xbox on the same network since you can't forward the same ports or range of ports to the other Xbox. So the Xbox Live service works fine but gaming with 2 of the exact same Xbox consoles while playing the exact same game becomes very problematic. Currently using pf with FreeBSD won't allow me to play with my son on separate Xbox consoles. One of us wouldn't be able to connect to the gaming server. Please review the website below reference how symmetric NAT is problematic for gaming:

http://badmodems.com/Forum/viewtopic.php?t=21

The patch should allow upnp (miniupnpd for example) to work properly and allow the same type of gaming consoles, to play the exact game at the same time, using the same ports on the same network using the upnp protocol. Currently, miniupnpd will only work with a single Xbox without issues but any additional Xbox on the same network has problems.

Both pfSense and OPNsense has these gaming problems because of pf. It's been an uphill battle for pfSense to figure this issue out for over 10 years.

See below reference and attempt to sort out miniupnpd to properly work with pfSense:

https://redmine.pfsense.org/issues/7727#change-41170

https://forum.netgate.com/topic/154153/test-request-upnp-fix-for-multiple-consoles-playing-the-same-game-static-port-outbound-nat

Hope this sheds some light on the issue and also why these patches are needed.
